title: SYNGAP1 Synchronizes Relative Neuronal Maturation Across Cortical Areas to Organize Distributed Functional Networks
abstract: SYNGAP1 haploinsufficiency produces opposing cortical activity patterns: reduced sensory-evoked gain and amplified movement/arousal activity. Layer 2/3 populations across cortical regions occupy distinct maturation states. Perturbing SYNGAP1 reduces developmental separability through region-dependent, opposite-direction effects on dendritic maturation.
